duration: 1 hour on this the first observation
description: A medium sized gull, with medium gray mantle, dark M pattern on extended upper wings with white ends to secondaries and inner primaries, creating a white trailing edge to most of the extended wing. Outer few primaries were dark, coupled with dark carpal bar creating the bold M pattern on the upper wing. Underwing was white. Bill and leg color were black. A dark spot appeared behind and low of the eye, and a dark neck-boa was evident on an otherwise white head and neck. Tail was white with a thin, dark terminal band.Behaviors: Flying and feeding in open water of Cherry Creek reservoir. Bird would pick morsals from near the surface and continue flying or land in the water.Call: none notedPlumage: juvenile 1st winter
